requirements.summary: Add a 'Delete Account' button in the user profile menu that opens a confirmation dialog and permanently deletes the user's account, clearing all local data and logging out
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Delete Account' button is visible in the user profile menu or settings area
Clicking the button opens a modal confirmation dialog with a clear warning that deletion is permanent
The dialog has a clearly labeled destructive confirm button (e.g., 'Yes, Delete My Account') and a cancel button
On confirmation, the backend `deleteAccount` function is invoked
All user-related keys in localStorage (profile, complaints metadata, notifications, alerts) are cleared on successful deletion
The user is logged out via the Internet Identity logout flow after deletion
The user is redirected to the landing/login page after successful deletion
If the backend call fails, an error message is shown and the account is not deleted

file_operations.description: Add a `useDeleteAccount` mutation hook that calls the backend `deleteAccount` function and returns a TanStack Query mutation for invoking account deletion with proper error handling

Create a confirmation dialog component using Shadcn UI AlertDialog that displays a warning message about permanent deletion, provides a destructive 'Yes, Delete My Account' button and a 'Cancel' button, invokes the `useDeleteAccount` mutation on confirmation, clears all localStorage keys (complaints metadata, notifications, alerts, user profile data), logs out via Internet Identity clear function, and redirects to the landing page on success. Display error messages if the backend call fails

Add a 'Delete Account' button with a destructive red text style in the user profile dropdown menu (within the DropdownMenuContent after the existing Logout item), and wire it to open the DeleteAccountDialog component when clicked
